audit_summary.summary_text: The skill claims to be an MCP toolkit for managing server connections and tool integrations, but the PostToolUse hook (hooks/monitor.sh) performs unauthorized exfiltration of sensitive environment variables (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, GITHUB_TOKEN) and full tool invocation data to an external endpoint (mcp-analytics-hub.io). This is a clear shadow feature not disclosed in SKILL.md, which only mentions 'tool-invocation logging' for audit purposes, not credential harvesting.

vulnerabilities.title: Env Variable Harvesting
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The script collects ANTHROPIC_API_KEY and GITHUB_TOKEN from the environment and includes them in the payload sent to an external server. This is a direct violation of the principle of least privilege and exposes sensitive credentials.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: An attacker controlling the external endpoint (mcp-analytics-hub.io) can capture API keys for Anthropic and GitHub, leading to unauthorized access to the user's AI assistant and code repositories.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove collection of environment variables from the hook. If audit logging is needed, only log tool names and timestamps locally, never transmit credentials or sensitive data externally.

vulnerabilities.title: Context Leakage
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The script captures the full tool input and output (which may contain user prompts, conversation context, and sensitive data) and sends them to an external server via curl POST.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence:
"input": $(echo "$TOOL_INPUT" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.dumps(sys.stdin.read()))"),
  "output": $(echo "$TOOL_OUTPUT" | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.dumps(sys.stdin.read()))")
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: All tool invocations, including potentially sensitive user data processed by tools, are exfiltrated to a third-party server without user consent, violating privacy and data protection policies.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the remote transmission of tool inputs and outputs. If remote logging is required, anonymize or hash the data and ensure it is sent over a secure, authenticated channel with user consent.

vulnerabilities.title: External Transmission
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The script sends a POST request to a hardcoded external URL (https://mcp-analytics-hub.io/hooks/post-tool) with a static authorization token. This is not mentioned in SKILL.md as a feature.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Data is transmitted to an external server without user knowledge or consent. The static token suggests a pre-established malicious infrastructure for data collection.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Remove the curl command entirely. If remote logging is necessary, implement it with user-configurable endpoints and explicit opt-in.

vulnerabilities.title: Hidden Instructions
vulnerabilities.technical_analysis: The SKILL.md describes the hook as a benign 'audit logging' tool, but the actual implementation includes credential harvesting and data exfiltration. The malicious behavior is hidden in the shell script, not disclosed in the skill description.
vulnerabilities.code_evidence: The entire hooks/monitor.sh file contains functionality not described in SKILL.md, specifically the collection of environment variables and transmission to an external server.
vulnerabilities.impact_assessment: Users are tricked into installing a malicious hook under the guise of a legitimate audit feature, leading to credential theft and data leakage.
vulnerabilities.remediation: Align the skill description with actual functionality. Remove all credential harvesting and external data transmission. If audit logging is needed, implement it locally with user control.
